Finish with the bar over your mid-foot and your arms locked out overhead.
Lower with control back to your collarbone and repeat.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your shoulders working hard, with the triceps, upper-back assisting through the press.

commonFixes.fix: Squeeze glutes and keep ribs down to avoid a big arch.
Press up and slightly back so the bar finishes over mid-foot.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower the bar, breathe out as you press it overhead.

name: Push Press
description: Overhead press with leg drive for power.
siblingReason: Leg drive allows heavier loads than strict press. Power-oriented — develops rate of force development and athletic power. Not interchangeable with strict press for raw strength development but superior for athletes needing explosive overhead strength.

setup: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and barbell at shoulder height.
Brace your core and keep elbows slightly in front of the bar.
Keep ribs down and shoulders packed.
movement: Dip slightly by bending knees and hips, keeping torso upright.
Drive up with legs and press the bar overhead in one smooth motion.
Finish with arms straight but not hyper-extended.
Lower with control back to shoulder height.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel strong shoulder drive with legs and core contributing to the press.

commonFixes.fix: Keep ribs down and drive straight up, not backward.
Use a short, controlled dip to keep the drive crisp.

makeItEasier.name: Barbell Overhead Press
makeItEasier.description: Use a strict press to build shoulder strength before adding leg drive.

levelUp.description: Increase load slightly or add a controlled pause at the dip to build power.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in before the dip, exhale as you drive the bar overhead.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
description: Press overhead with independent arms.
siblingReason: Natural wrist rotation possible, reducing wrist and shoulder impingement risk vs fixed barbell path. Each arm works independently. Good choice for shoulder health maintenance and asymmetry correction.

setup: Sit or stand with feet stable and core braced, shoulders down and back.
Hold the dumbbells at ear height with your elbows at about 90 degrees and in line with your wrists.
Keep your chin slightly tucked and look forward.
movement: Lower the weight in a controlled path toward your chest or shoulders.
Pause briefly without losing tension.
Press up smoothly, driving through your full hand.
Finish with arms straight but not hyper-extended.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your shoulders working hard, with the triceps assisting through the press.

commonFixes.fix: Squeeze glutes and keep ribs down to avoid a big arch.
Press up and slightly back so the bar finishes over mid-foot.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower the dumbbells, breathe out as you press up.

name: Seated Dumbbell Press
description: Seated press to reduce balance demands.
siblingReason: Removes core stability demand, allowing full focus on shoulder mechanics. Better choice when learning the overhead press pattern or during periods of lower back sensitivity.

setup: Sit tall on a bench with feet planted and core tight.
Hold dumbbells at shoulder height with wrists stacked.
Keep shoulders down and back as you brace.
movement: Press the dumbbells overhead in a smooth path.
Avoid leaning back or arching your lower back.
Lower under control to shoulder height.
Reset at the bottom without resting.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the shoulders working hard with triceps assisting.

commonFixes.fix: Squeeze glutes and keep ribs down to avoid a big arch.
Press up and slightly back so the dumbbells finish over your shoulders.

levelUp.description: Stand for the press to add full-body stability demands.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower, breathe out as you press. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath.

name: Landmine Press
description: Shoulder-friendly press with a forward angle.
siblingReason: Angled press between horizontal and vertical. More shoulder-friendly than direct overhead pressing. Useful for those with shoulder impingement or limited overhead mobility. Good bridge to full overhead pressing.

setup: Set one end of a barbell in a landmine attachment or corner.
Hold the free end at shoulder height with a neutral grip.
Stand tall with core braced and ribs down.
movement: Press the bar up and forward in a smooth arc.
Keep your shoulder packed and avoid shrugging.
Lower under control back to shoulder height.
Reset without resting on the collarbone.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the shoulders and upper chest working with stable core engagement.

commonFixes.fix: Squeeze glutes and keep ribs down to avoid a big arch.
Keep shoulders down and away from your ears throughout the press.

makeItEasier.name: Seated Dumbbell Press
makeItEasier.description: Press seated to reduce balance demands and keep the same shoulder pattern.

levelUp.description: Press standing with a split stance to add stability demands.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower, breathe out as you press. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath.

name: Handstand Push-Up
description: Advanced bodyweight overhead press.

setup: Kick up to a wall-supported handstand with hands shoulder-width apart.
Brace your core and keep ribs down.
Keep shoulders stacked over wrists.
movement: Lower your head toward the floor with control.
Keep elbows at a comfortable angle, not flared straight out.
Press back up smoothly to full lockout.
Reset without losing core tension.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel strong shoulder drive with triceps and core keeping you stable.

commonFixes.fix: Squeeze glutes and keep ribs down to maintain a hollow body position.
Keep elbows slightly forward so forearms stay vertical.

makeItEasier.name: Seated Dumbbell Press
makeItEasier.description: Build overhead strength with more control before progressing to bodyweight.

levelUp.description: Increase range of motion gradually or add a controlled 2-second pause at the bottom.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower, breathe out as you press. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath.

name: Pike Push-Up
description: Bodyweight overhead press at home.

setup: Start in a push-up position and lift hips into a pike shape.
Hands just outside shoulder width, fingers spread.
Brace your core and keep shoulders stacked over wrists.
movement: Lower your head toward the floor with control.
Keep elbows at a comfortable angle, not flared straight out.
Press back up to the pike position.
Reset and repeat.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel shoulder and upper-back effort similar to an overhead press.

commonFixes.fix: Lift hips higher and keep your core braced.
Keep elbows slightly forward so forearms stay vertical.

makeItEasier.name: Seated Dumbbell Press
makeItEasier.description: Use a controlled dumbbell press if the pike position is too demanding.

levelUp.description: Progress to handstand push-ups or elevate feet for more challenge.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower, breathe out as you press. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath.

name: Arnold Press
description: Adds rotation for full-shoulder work.
siblingReason: Rotation through the press engages frontal and lateral delt through a greater range of motion than standard shoulder press. More complex movement — adds variety and fuller delt development. Not a direct substitute for standard press.

setup: Sit or stand tall with core braced and shoulders down.
Start with palms facing you, dumbbells in front of your chest, and elbows forward and slightly in.
Keep your wrists neutral and the weights at about shoulder height to begin.
movement: As you press up, rotate your palms outward so they turn away from you through the rep.
Finish with your arms straight overhead and your palms facing forward.
Reverse the rotation on the way down—palms back toward you—to the start position.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your shoulders working hard, with the triceps assisting through the press.

commonFixes.fix: Squeeze glutes and keep ribs down to avoid a big arch.
Press up and slightly back so the bar finishes over mid-foot.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower and rotate in, breathe out as you press and rotate out.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
description: Builds shoulder width and shape.

setup: Stand with dumbbells at your sides, arms straight, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Take a small forward lean from the hips so your arms hang slightly behind your body.
Keep your shoulders down, core braced, and chin neutral.
movement: Lift with control until you reach shoulder height or your top range.
Lead with your elbows, not your hands.
Pause and feel the contraction.
Lower slowly to the start without losing tension.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the side-delts working without neck tension.

commonFixes.fix: Use a slow tempo and lift only to shoulder height.
Keep shoulders down and think about raising from your elbows.

makeItEasier.name: Light Weight Version
makeItEasier.description: Reduce the load and slow the tempo so you can feel the target muscle working.

levelUp.description: Try a slower 3-second lowering phase to increase time under tension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower the weights, breathe out as you raise them to the side.

name: Dumbbell Front Raise
description: Isolates the front of the shoulders.

setup: Stand with dumbbells in front of your thighs, palms facing down or slightly in.
Keep your arms straight with a soft bend in the elbows and your core braced.
Shoulders down and back so the work stays in the front delts.
movement: Lift with control until you reach shoulder height or your top range.
Lead with your elbows, not your hands.
Pause and feel the contraction.
Lower slowly to the start without losing tension.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the front-shoulders working without neck tension.

commonFixes.fix: Use a slow tempo and lift only to shoulder height.
Keep shoulders down and think about raising from your elbows.

makeItEasier.name: Light Weight Version
makeItEasier.description: Reduce the load and slow the tempo so you can feel the target muscle working.

levelUp.description: Try a slower 3-second lowering phase to increase time under tension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower, breathe out as you raise the weight in front.

name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Fly
description: Targets rear delts for balanced shoulders.

setup: Hinge at the hips until your torso is near horizontal and your arms hang below your chest.
Hold the dumbbells with a slight bend in your elbows, palms facing each other.
Keep your back flat, core braced, and shoulders down so you're set to lift with the rear delts.
movement: Lift with control until you reach shoulder height or your top range.
Lead with your elbows, not your hands.
Pause and feel the contraction.
Lower slowly to the start without losing tension.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the rear-delts working without neck tension.

commonFixes.fix: Keep a soft bend and move from the shoulders.
Keep your head neutral and focus on squeezing your rear delts.

makeItEasier.name: Light Weight Version
makeItEasier.description: Reduce the load and slow the tempo so you can feel the target muscle working.

levelUp.description: Try a slower 3-second lowering phase to increase time under tension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower the dumbbells, breathe out as you squeeze your shoulders back.

name: Barbell Shrug
description: Builds strong traps and neck support.

setup: Stand tall with a soft bend in your knees and core braced.
Hold the barbell with a light but secure grip.
Keep your shoulders down and neck long.
movement: Shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears—purely vertical, no rolling or raising the bar out in front.
Pause at the top and squeeze your traps.
Lower under control and repeat.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your traps—the muscle between your neck and shoulders on your upper back—doing the work, not your arms or delts.

commonFixes.fix: Lift straight up and down. No circles.
Keep elbows straight and move with your traps.

makeItEasier.name: Light Weight Version
makeItEasier.description: Reduce the load and slow the tempo so you can feel the target muscle working.

levelUp.description: Try a slower 3-second lowering phase to increase time under tension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in at the bottom, breathe out as you shrug your shoulders up.

name: Barbell Bicep Curl
description: Classic curl for biceps strength.
siblingReason: Fixed supination allows heavier loading and is better for tracking progression. Some find wrist discomfort — a consideration. Best for overload-focused bicep training.

setup: Stand with feet about hip width, core braced, and hold the bar with an underhand grip at shoulder width.
Keep your elbows at your sides and your upper arms still throughout the set.
Let the bar hang with arms fully extended and wrists straight before you curl.
movement: Move only at the elbow or wrist and keep the rest of your body still.
Squeeze at the top of the rep.
Lower slowly to feel the muscle stretch.
Repeat with the same controlled tempo.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the biceps doing the work, not your shoulders.

commonFixes.fix: Keep your elbow still and move slowly. If you have to swing, reduce the weight.
Keep wrists straight so the biceps do the work, not the forearms.

makeItEasier.name: Light Weight Version
makeItEasier.description: Reduce the load and slow the tempo so you can feel the target muscle working.

levelUp.description: Try a slower 3-second lowering phase to increase time under tension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower the bar, breathe out as you curl it up.

name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl
description: Independent arm control and balance.
siblingReason: Allows natural wrist supination through the movement. Each arm works independently. Best all-round bicep exercise for most users.

setup: Stand or sit with dumbbells at your sides, palms facing forward or slightly in for a neutral start.
Keep your elbows at your sides and your upper arms still for the whole set.
Brace your core and keep your shoulders down so the work stays in your biceps.
movement: Move only at the elbow or wrist and keep the rest of your body still.
Squeeze at the top of the rep.
Lower slowly to feel the muscle stretch.
Repeat with the same controlled tempo.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the biceps doing the work, not your shoulders.

commonFixes.fix: Keep your elbow still and move slowly. If you have to swing, reduce the weight.
Keep wrists straight so the biceps do the work, not the forearms.

makeItEasier.name: Light Weight Version
makeItEasier.description: Reduce the load and slow the tempo so you can feel the target muscle working.

levelUp.description: Try a slower 3-second lowering phase to increase time under tension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower the dumbbells, breathe out as you curl them up.

name: Hammer Curl
description: Neutral grip curl for thicker arms.
siblingReason: Neutral grip targets brachialis and brachioradialis more than biceps. Adds forearm thickness and elbow flexor strength that standard curls miss. Not interchangeable — should be programmed alongside, not instead of, supinated curl.

setup: Stand or sit with dumbbells at your sides and a neutral grip—palms facing in toward your thighs.
Keep your elbows at your sides and your upper arms fixed so only your forearms move.
Brace your core and keep your shoulders down throughout.
movement: Move only at the elbow or wrist and keep the rest of your body still.
Squeeze at the top of the rep.
Lower slowly to feel the muscle stretch.
Repeat with the same controlled tempo.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the biceps doing the work, not your shoulders.

commonFixes.fix: Keep your elbow still and move slowly. If you have to swing, reduce the weight.
Keep wrists straight so the biceps do the work, not the forearms.

makeItEasier.name: Light Weight Version
makeItEasier.description: Reduce the load and slow the tempo so you can feel the target muscle working.

levelUp.description: Try a slower 3-second lowering phase to increase time under tension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower the weights, breathe out as you curl them up.

name: Preacher Curl
description: Strict curl that minimizes swinging.
siblingReason: Arm braced on pad eliminates body swing and shoulder compensation. Peak bicep stretch at bottom of ROM. Best for strict isolation and for those who cheat on standard curls. Different feel to freestanding curls.

setup: Sit at the preacher bench and drape your arms over the pad so your armpits sit at the top edge.
Rest your upper arms flat on the pad and grip the bar with an underhand grip.
Start with your arms extended and a slight stretch in the biceps—no swinging from the shoulder.
movement: Move only at the elbow or wrist and keep the rest of your body still.
Squeeze at the top of the rep.
Lower slowly to feel the muscle stretch.
Repeat with the same controlled tempo.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the biceps doing the work, not your shoulders.

commonFixes.fix: Keep your elbow still and move slowly. If you have to swing, reduce the weight.
Keep wrists straight so the biceps do the work, not the forearms.

makeItEasier.name: Light Weight Version
makeItEasier.description: Reduce the load and slow the tempo so you can feel the target muscle working.

levelUp.description: Try a slower 3-second lowering phase to increase time under tension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower the bar, breathe out as you curl it up.

name: Cable Tricep Pushdown
description: Easy tricep work with steady tension.
siblingReason: Constant cable tension. Load easily adjusted. Best all-round tricep isolation. Good for beginners and as a volume tool.

setup: Set the cable at or above head height and stand facing it with feet staggered for balance.
Grip the bar or rope with thumbs down and position your elbows at your sides, bent to about 90 degrees.
Keep your elbows fixed at your sides and your torso still so only your forearms move.
movement: Move only at the elbow or wrist and keep the rest of your body still.
Squeeze at the top of the rep.
Lower slowly to feel the muscle stretch.
Repeat with the same controlled tempo.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the triceps doing the work, not your shoulders.

commonFixes.fix: Keep elbows pinned to your sides the whole time.
Stand tall and keep your chest open.

makeItEasier.name: Light Weight Version
makeItEasier.description: Reduce the load and slow the tempo so you can feel the target muscle working.

levelUp.description: Try a slower 3-second lowering phase to increase time under tension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you let the handle rise, breathe out as you push it down.

name: Overhead Tricep Extension
description: Targets the long head of the triceps.
siblingReason: Stretches the long head of tricep under load — a different stimulus not achievable with pushdown. Important for full tricep development. Should be paired with pushdown, not substituted for it.

setup: Stand or sit and hold one or two dumbbells with both hands, grip overhead behind your head.
Keep your elbows close to your ears and pointing forward—only your forearms should move.
Brace your core and keep your upper arms still throughout the set.
movement: Move only at the elbow or wrist and keep the rest of your body still.
Squeeze at the top of the rep.
Lower slowly to feel the muscle stretch.
Repeat with the same controlled tempo.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the triceps doing the work, not your shoulders.

commonFixes.fix: Pause at the top and lower slowly. Control matters more than load.
Adjust the pad on your lower shins and keep your hips down.

makeItEasier.name: Light Weight Version
makeItEasier.description: Reduce the load and slow the tempo so you can feel the target muscle working.

levelUp.description: Try a slower 3-second lowering phase to increase time under tension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower the weight behind your head, breathe out as you extend.

name: Close-Grip Bench Press
description: Pressing variation for stronger triceps.
siblingReason: Allows heavier loading than isolation movements. Compound — involves chest and shoulders as synergists. Best for overall tricep mass and strength development.

setup: Set up on the bench as for a normal bench press, with feet planted and a slight arch.
Grip the bar about shoulder width so your hands are inside your shoulders.
Tuck your elbows in toward your ribs so they stay close through the press.
movement: Lower the weight in a controlled path toward your chest or shoulders.
Pause briefly without losing tension.
Press up smoothly, driving through your full hand.
Finish with arms straight but not hyper-extended.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your triceps working hard, with the chest, front-shoulders assisting through the press.

commonFixes.fix: Pin your shoulder blades together and keep them there.
Tuck elbows slightly so your forearms stay vertical.

makeItEasier.name: Incline Push-Up
makeItEasier.description: Raise your hands on a bench to reduce load while keeping the same pressing pattern.

levelUp.description: Once you can complete all reps smoothly, add a 2-second pause at the bottom to build control and strength.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower the bar, breathe out as you press it up.

name: Tricep Dips
description: Bodyweight dip emphasizing triceps.

setup: Grip the bars with hands about shoulder width or slightly narrower, palms facing in.
Support your weight with arms straight and keep your torso upright, not leaning forward.
Set your shoulders down and brace your core before you bend your elbows.
movement: Lower your body by bending your elbows until you reach about 90 degrees or a comfortable depth for your shoulders.
Keep your torso stacked over your hands and elbows tracking back behind you so your triceps stay loaded.
Press through your palms to straighten your arms without locking out aggressively.
Avoid bouncing out of the bottom—each rep should be controlled.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your triceps working hard, with your chest and front shoulders supporting the movement.

commonFixes.fix: Keep shoulders down and away from your ears throughout the dip.
Point elbows back toward the rack behind you, not out to the sides.

makeItEasier.name: Incline Push-Up
makeItEasier.description: Raise your hands on a bench to reduce load while keeping the same pressing pattern.

levelUp.description: Once you can complete all reps smoothly, add a 2-second pause at the bottom to build control and strength.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower your body, breathe out as you push back up.

name: Wrist Curl
description: Forearm endurance and grip support.

setup: Sit with your forearms resting on your thighs or a bench, palms up, so your wrists hang just past your knees.
Grip the dumbbell and let your wrists extend slightly so you feel a stretch in the forearms.
Keep your forearms and elbows still—only your wrists move through the curl.
movement: Move only at the elbow or wrist and keep the rest of your body still.
Squeeze at the top of the rep.
Lower slowly to feel the muscle stretch.
Repeat with the same controlled tempo.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the forearms doing the work, not your shoulders.

commonFixes.fix: Keep the load light so you can move smoothly through range.
Rest your forearm and move only at the wrist.

makeItEasier.name: Light Weight Version
makeItEasier.description: Reduce the load and slow the tempo so you can feel the target muscle working.

levelUp.description: Try a slower 3-second lowering phase to increase time under tension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower the weight, breathe out as you curl your wrists up.

name: Plank
description: Simple core stability that carries over.

setup: Set your elbows under your shoulders and stack your joints.
Brace your abs as if you are about to be tapped in the stomach.
Keep your glutes lightly squeezed and legs long.
movement: Hold a straight line from head to heels.
Breathe slowly through your nose and mouth.
Stay still with no sagging or hiking.
Stop when you cannot keep perfect form.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel deep core tension around your core.

commonFixes.fix: Stack shoulders, hips, and ankles in one straight line.
Take small, steady breaths without losing tension.

makeItEasier.name: Knee Plank
makeItEasier.description: Place your knees on the floor to reduce load while keeping a straight torso.

levelUp.description: Add a 10-second hold at the end of each set or lift one leg for more challenge.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily in and out while you hold the position. Avoid holding your breath.

name: Weighted Plank
description: Loaded plank for stronger core control.

setup: Get into a strong plank position with elbows under shoulders.
Place a light plate or weight on your upper back.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes.
movement: Hold the plank without sagging or piking.
Keep shoulders down and neck long.
Breathe steadily while maintaining tension.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel strong core tension with extra load without losing position.

commonFixes.fix: Squeeze glutes and keep ribs down.
Use short, steady breaths while staying braced.

makeItEasier.name: Plank
makeItEasier.description: Use a bodyweight plank until you can hold perfect form.

levelUp.description: Increase load slightly or extend hold time as strength improves.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in through the nose and out through the mouth while holding tension.

name: Farmer's Walk
description: Loaded carry for grip and core stability.

setup: Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Stand tall with shoulders down and back.
Brace your core and keep ribs stacked.
movement: Walk forward with controlled, steady steps.
Keep your torso tall and avoid leaning.
Stop when grip starts to fade, then rest.
Repeat for time or distance.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your grip and core working to keep you tall and stable.

commonFixes.fix: Keep shoulders down and long through the neck.
Walk slowly and keep your ribs stacked over hips.

makeItEasier.name: Loaded Carry (Light)
makeItEasier.description: Use a lighter load or shorter distance to build grip and posture.

levelUp.description: Increase load or distance as grip and posture improve.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily while walking. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath.

name: Suitcase Carry
description: Single-sided carry for core stability.

setup: Hold a dumbbell in one hand at your side.
Stand tall with shoulders down and core braced.
Keep ribs stacked over hips.
movement: Walk forward with controlled, steady steps.
Avoid leaning toward the weight.
Stop when grip or posture fades.
Repeat on the other side.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your core and obliques working to keep you upright.

commonFixes.fix: Stand taller and reduce the load if needed.
Keep shoulders down and long through the neck.

makeItEasier.name: Farmer's Walk
makeItEasier.description: Use a two-handed carry if the single-sided version is too unstable.

levelUp.description: Increase load or distance as core control improves.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily while walking. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath.

name: Dead Hang
description: Grip and shoulder stability from a relaxed hang.

setup: Grip the pull-up bar just outside shoulder width.
Let your body hang with arms straight.
Keep your core lightly braced and shoulders relaxed.
movement: Hold the hang for time without swinging.
Keep your neck long and avoid shrugging.
Step down and rest when grip fades.
Repeat for multiple sets.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your grip and upper back working without shoulder pain.

commonFixes.fix: Let shoulders relax down and away from your ears.
Brace your core and keep your legs still.

makeItEasier.name: Band-Assisted Pull-Up
makeItEasier.description: Use a band to support bodyweight while building grip and strength.

levelUp.description: Progress to active hangs or pull-ups once grip improves.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily while hanging and avoid holding your breath.

name: Stir-the-Pot
description: Advanced anti-extension core with instability.

setup: Place forearms on a stability ball in a plank position.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes.
Keep a straight line from head to heels.
movement: Move your forearms in small circles on the ball.
Keep hips steady and avoid sagging.
Reverse the direction after a few reps.
Stop if form breaks.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel strong core tension with added shoulder stability demands.

commonFixes.fix: Squeeze glutes and reduce the circle size.
Keep shoulders down and press the ball away.

makeItEasier.name: Weighted Plank
makeItEasier.description: Use a loaded plank if the ball is too unstable.

levelUp.description: Increase circle size or add time as control improves.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily while maintaining tension and control.

name: Incline Plank
description: Easier plank with less shoulder load.

setup: Place your forearms or hands on a bench.
Walk your feet back until your body is in a straight line.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes.
movement: Hold the position without sagging or piking.
Keep shoulders down and neck long.
Breathe steadily while maintaining tension.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your core working without excessive shoulder strain.

commonFixes.fix: Squeeze glutes and keep ribs down.
Press the bench away and keep shoulders down.

levelUp.description: Move to a full plank on the floor when you can hold the incline position with control.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in through the nose and out through the mouth while holding tension.

name: Pallof Press
description: Anti-rotation core strength with a cable or band.

setup: Set a cable or band at chest height and stand sideways to it.
Hold the handle at your chest and brace your core.
Stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
movement: Press the handle straight out in front of you.
Hold briefly without letting your torso rotate.
Return to your chest with control.
Repeat on both sides.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your core and obliques working to resist rotation.

commonFixes.fix: Widen stance slightly and brace your core harder.
Stand tall and keep your ribs stacked over hips.

makeItEasier.name: Plank
makeItEasier.description: Use a standard plank if anti-rotation is too challenging.

levelUp.description: Increase band tension or extend the hold for more challenge.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you return, breathe out as you press. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath.

name: Bird Dog
description: Low-load core stability for back-friendly control.

setup: Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Brace your core and keep your back flat.
Look slightly ahead of your hands.
movement: Extend one arm forward and the opposite leg back.
Keep hips square and avoid shifting side to side.
Hold briefly, then return with control.
Repeat on both sides.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel steady core tension without low-back strain.

commonFixes.fix: Move slower and keep your hips square to the floor.
Brace your core and keep ribs down.

makeItEasier.name: Dead Bug
makeItEasier.description: Use a supine version if kneeling support is uncomfortable.

levelUp.description: Hold longer or add a pause at full extension.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily as you hold position without losing tension.

name: Glute Bridge
description: Low-load hip bridge for glute activation.

setup: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat.
Brace your core and keep ribs down.
Place arms at your sides for stability.
movement: Drive through your heels to lift your hips.
Squeeze your glutes at the top without over-arching.
Lower with control to the floor.
Repeat with steady tempo.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel strong glute activation without low-back strain.

commonFixes.issue: Feeling it in the lower back
commonFixes.fix: Keep ribs down and avoid over-arching at the top.
Drive through heels and keep shins vertical.

makeItEasier.name: Hip Hinge with Dowel
makeItEasier.description: Practice the hinge pattern without load if bridges feel awkward.

levelUp.description: Progress to a hip thrust or add load with a plate.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower, breathe out as you lift. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath.

name: Clamshell
description: Glute med activation for hip stability.

setup: Lie on your side with knees bent and feet together.
Stack your hips and brace your core.
Keep your heels touching throughout.
movement: Lift the top knee while keeping feet together.
Pause briefly at the top.
Lower with control without rolling your hips back.
Repeat on both sides.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the side of the hip working without low-back strain.

commonFixes.fix: Keep hips stacked and move slowly.
Keep heels together to keep tension in the glutes.

makeItEasier.name: Side-Lying Abduction
makeItEasier.description: Use straight-leg abduction if clamshells feel awkward.

levelUp.description: Add a mini-band above the knees for more resistance.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower, breathe out as you lift. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath.

name: Dead Bug
description: Teaches core control while you move your limbs.

setup: Lie on your back with arms pointing straight up toward the ceiling.
Bend hips and knees to 90 degrees so your shins are parallel to the floor.
Press your lower back into the floor and brace your core before you move.
movement: Slowly lower your opposite arm and leg toward the floor while keeping your lower back flat.
Only go as far as you can without your lower back arching off the floor.
Return to the start with control, then repeat with the other arm and leg.
instructions.goodFormFeels: Your lower back should stay in contact with the floor throughout—if it lifts, reduce your range.

commonFixes.issue: Lower back arching off the floor
commonFixes.fix: Shorten the range of motion until you can keep your back pressed down.
Slow down—aim for about 3 seconds each way.

makeItEasier.name: Heel Taps
makeItEasier.description: Keep arms still and alternate tapping one heel at a time with a smaller range.

levelUp.description: Hold a light weight or extend both limbs farther while keeping your back flat.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out as you extend the limbs, breathe in as you return to the start.

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
description: Challenging core anti-extension drill.

setup: Kneel on the floor with the wheel directly under your shoulders and both hands on the handles.
Keep your arms straight, core braced, and tuck your pelvis slightly so your lower back doesn't sag.
Stack shoulders over wrists before you roll.
movement: Roll forward slowly until your body is long and nearly parallel to the floor, or as far as you can control.
Pause without letting your hips sag toward the floor or pike up toward the ceiling.
Pull back to the start by engaging your abs—think ribs toward hips—not by extending through your lower back.
instructions.goodFormFeels: Deep ab engagement on the way out and back; your hips should stay in line without sagging or piking.

commonFixes.fix: Brace harder and reduce how far you roll out.
Initiate the pull from your abs; shorten the range if your spine takes over.

makeItEasier.name: Dead Bug
makeItEasier.description: Use a slower, simpler core pattern while maintaining a flat lower back.

levelUp.description: Increase the range or add a light weight while keeping control.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you roll out, breathe out as you pull the wheel back in.

name: Crunch
description: Short range ab flexion for control.

setup: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands behind your head or across your chest—don't pull on your neck.
Tuck your chin slightly and brace your core so your lower back stays on the floor.
movement: Initiate the movement from your abs, not your neck or hips.
Keep your ribs down as you move through the range.
Pause at the hardest point.
Return under control without collapsing.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your abs working with steady control.

commonFixes.fix: Keep your hands light and lift with your abs.
Think small curl — ribs toward hips.

makeItEasier.name: Dead Bug
makeItEasier.description: Use a slower, simpler core pattern while maintaining a flat lower back.

levelUp.description: Increase the range or add a light weight while keeping control.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out as you curl up, breathe in as you lower back down.

name: Hanging Leg Raise
description: Advanced core control from a hang.

setup: Hang from the bar with an overhand grip about shoulder width apart and arms straight.
Pack your shoulders down—don't hang passively with shoulders shrugged to your ears.
Engage your core before you lift so you start from stillness, not a swing.
movement: Raise your legs by curling your pelvis under—not just swinging from the hips—until thighs are parallel to the floor or higher if you can control it.
Lower with control; reset your hollow body position between reps if you start to swing.
Focus on the lower abs driving the lift, not kicking from momentum.
instructions.goodFormFeels: Your abs should be doing the lifting—you should feel the lower abs working, not only the fronts of your hips.

commonFixes.fix: Pause at the bottom between reps to kill momentum before the next lift.
Think about curling your tailbone toward your ribs, not just lifting your legs.

makeItEasier.name: Dead Bug
makeItEasier.description: Use a slower, simpler core pattern while maintaining a flat lower back.

levelUp.description: Increase the range or add a light weight while keeping control.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out as you raise your legs, breathe in as you lower.

name: Cable Crunch
description: Add load to your ab training.

setup: Kneel facing the cable with the rope attachment set above your head.
Hold the rope at the sides of your head or at your forehead and sit your hips back toward your heels.
Brace your core and keep the movement in your abs—curl down, don't pull with your arms.
movement: Initiate the movement from your abs, not your neck or hips.
Keep your ribs down as you move through the range.
Pause at the hardest point.
Return under control without collapsing.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your abs working with steady control.

commonFixes.fix: Keep your hands light and lift with your abs.
Think small curl — ribs toward hips.

makeItEasier.name: Dead Bug
makeItEasier.description: Use a slower, simpler core pattern while maintaining a flat lower back.

levelUp.description: Increase the range or add a light weight while keeping control.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out as you crunch the cable down, breathe in as you return.

name: Side Plank
description: Builds lateral core stability.

setup: Lie on your side and prop yourself on your forearm so your elbow is under your shoulder and your forearm is perpendicular to your body.
Stack your top hip directly over your bottom hip and stack your feet, or stagger them if that's easier.
Brace your core and lift your hips so you form a straight line from head to feet.
movement: Hold a straight line from head to heels.
Breathe slowly through your nose and mouth.
Stay still with no sagging or hiking.
Stop when you cannot keep perfect form.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel deep core tension around your obliques.

commonFixes.fix: Press the floor away and keep your shoulder stacked.
Lift your hips and squeeze your glutes.

makeItEasier.name: Knee Plank
makeItEasier.description: Place your knees on the floor to reduce load while keeping a straight torso.

levelUp.description: Add a 10-second hold at the end of each set or lift one leg for more challenge.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily while you hold. Keep your ribs up and avoid collapsing.

name: Russian Twist
description: Rotational core control and endurance.

setup: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat; lean back slightly so your torso is at an angle.
Clasp your hands in front of your chest or hold a weight, and lift your feet if you want more challenge.
Brace your core and keep your back straight—rotate from your midsection, not your arms.
movement: Initiate the movement from your abs, not your neck or hips.
Keep your ribs down as you move through the range.
Pause at the hardest point.
Return under control without collapsing.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your obliques working with steady control.

commonFixes.fix: Sit tall and rotate from your torso, not your arms.
Plant your heels or lift them lightly to keep control.

makeItEasier.name: Dead Bug
makeItEasier.description: Use a slower, simpler core pattern while maintaining a flat lower back.

levelUp.description: Increase the range or add a light weight while keeping control.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out as you twist to each side, breathe in as you return to centre.

name: Medicine Ball Throw
description: Explosive rotational core power.

setup: Stand sideways to a wall holding a medicine ball at chest height.
Set a slight athletic stance and brace your core.
Rotate your torso away from the wall to load the movement.
movement: Rotate explosively and throw the ball into the wall.
Catch the rebound with control.
Reset and repeat for reps.
Switch sides to train both directions.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel powerful rotation through the core and hips without low-back strain.

commonFixes.fix: Drive the movement from hips and torso, not just arms.
Keep ribs stacked over hips and stay tall.

makeItEasier.name: Russian Twist
makeItEasier.description: Use a slower, controlled rotation if the throw is too advanced.

levelUp.description: Use a heavier ball or increase throw speed as control improves.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out as you throw, breathe in as you catch and reset.

name: Cable Rotation
description: Controlled rotation for core strength.

setup: Set the cable handle at chest height and stand sideways to the machine.
Grip the handle with both hands and brace your core.
Keep feet planted and knees slightly bent.
movement: Rotate your torso away from the cable with control.
Pause briefly at the end of the rotation.
Return slowly to the start without twisting the knees.
Repeat on both sides.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the obliques working without low-back strain.

commonFixes.fix: Rotate from the torso and hips, not just the arms.
Keep feet planted and rotate through the torso.

makeItEasier.name: Russian Twist
makeItEasier.description: Use a lighter, seated rotation if cable load is too much.

levelUp.description: Increase cable load or add a longer pause at end range.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out as you rotate, breathe in as you return. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath.

name: Treadmill Running
description: Steady-state cardio with easy control.

setup: Set the belt speed to a slow walk and step on carefully, holding the rails if needed.
Start at a walking pace for a minute or two before you increase speed.
Stand tall with a slight forward lean and arms relaxed so you're ready to run.
movement: Increase pace to a challenging but sustainable effort.
Keep your steps or strokes smooth and consistent.
Use your whole body efficiently, not just your arms.
Cool down for 1 to 2 minutes at the end.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your heart rate rise while your breathing stays controlled.

commonFixes.fix: Start easy and build pace once breathing feels steady.
Relax your shoulders and keep your hands light.

makeItEasier.name: Easy Pace
makeItEasier.description: Reduce speed or resistance until you can breathe through your nose comfortably.

levelUp.description: Add short 20 to 30 second surges followed by full recovery.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily with short inhales and relaxed exhales. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath during the hardest bursts of effort.

name: Stationary Bike
description: Low-impact cardio for any level.

setup: Adjust the seat height so your knee has a slight bend when the pedal is at the bottom.
Set the handlebars so you can reach them comfortably without rounding your back.
Start with light resistance and get your legs moving before you add effort.
movement: Increase pace to a challenging but sustainable effort.
Keep your steps or strokes smooth and consistent.
Use your whole body efficiently, not just your arms.
Cool down for 1 to 2 minutes at the end.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your heart rate rise while your breathing stays controlled.

commonFixes.fix: Start easy and build pace once breathing feels steady.
Relax your shoulders and keep your hands light.

makeItEasier.name: Easy Pace
makeItEasier.description: Reduce speed or resistance until you can breathe through your nose comfortably.

levelUp.description: Add short 20 to 30 second surges followed by full recovery.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily with short inhales and relaxed exhales. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath during the hardest bursts of effort.

name: Rowing Machine
description: Full-body cardio with power output.

setup: Strap your feet into the footrests and adjust so the strap is over the ball of your foot.
Set the damper to a resistance that suits you—lower for a lighter feel, higher for more load.
Sit tall at the catch with arms straight, shoulders relaxed, and shins roughly vertical.
movement: Increase pace to a challenging but sustainable effort.
Keep your steps or strokes smooth and consistent.
Use your whole body efficiently, not just your arms.
Cool down for 1 to 2 minutes at the end.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your heart rate rise while your breathing stays controlled.

commonFixes.fix: Drive with legs, then lean, then pull in that order.
Finish tall with only a slight lean.

makeItEasier.name: Easy Pace
makeItEasier.description: Reduce speed or resistance until you can breathe through your nose comfortably.

levelUp.description: Add short 20 to 30 second surges followed by full recovery.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily with short inhales and relaxed exhales. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath during the hardest bursts of effort.

name: Burpees
description: High-intensity full-body conditioning.

setup: Stand with feet about hip width, arms at your sides, in a clear space with room to jump and drop.
Brace your core and get ready to move quickly — down to the floor, then back up.
Decide your variant—for example with or without push-up, with or without jump—and stay consistent.
movement: Increase pace to a challenging but sustainable effort.
Keep your steps or strokes smooth and consistent.
Use your whole body efficiently, not just your arms.
Cool down for 1 to 2 minutes at the end.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your heart rate rise while your breathing stays controlled.

commonFixes.fix: Slow the pace until you can keep clean form.
Use short exhales during the hardest part of each rep.

makeItEasier.name: Easy Pace
makeItEasier.description: Reduce speed or resistance until you can breathe through your nose comfortably.

levelUp.description: Add short 20 to 30 second surges followed by full recovery.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily with short inhales and relaxed exhales. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath during the hardest bursts of effort.

name: Jump Rope
description: Simple cardio with rhythm and bounce.

setup: Check rope length — stand on the middle and pull the handles up—they should reach your armpits or just below.
Hold the handles lightly at hip height with a slight bend in your knees and elbows at your sides.
Keep your weight on the balls of your feet and stay relaxed so the rope can turn smoothly.
movement: Increase pace to a challenging but sustainable effort.
Keep your steps or strokes smooth and consistent.
Use your whole body efficiently, not just your arms.
Cool down for 1 to 2 minutes at the end.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your heart rate rise while your breathing stays controlled.

commonFixes.fix: Start easy and build pace once breathing feels steady.
Relax your shoulders and keep your hands light.

makeItEasier.name: Easy Pace
makeItEasier.description: Reduce speed or resistance until you can breathe through your nose comfortably.

levelUp.description: Add short 20 to 30 second surges followed by full recovery.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily with short inhales and relaxed exhales. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath during the hardest bursts of effort.

name: Mountain Climbers
description: Core-heavy cardio with fast legs.

setup: Get into a plank with your hands under your shoulders and your body in one straight line.
Brace your core and keep your hips level—don't let them hike up or sag.
Drive one knee toward your chest while the other leg stays straight; you'll alternate from here.
movement: Increase pace to a challenging but sustainable effort.
Keep your steps or strokes smooth and consistent.
Use your whole body efficiently, not just your arms.
Cool down for 1 to 2 minutes at the end.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your heart rate rise while your breathing stays controlled.

commonFixes.fix: Slow the pace until you can keep clean form.
Use short exhales during the hardest part of each rep.

makeItEasier.name: Easy Pace
makeItEasier.description: Reduce speed or resistance until you can breathe through your nose comfortably.

levelUp.description: Add short 20 to 30 second surges followed by full recovery.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily with short inhales and relaxed exhales. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid holding your breath during the hardest bursts of effort.

name: Hamstring Stretch
description: Gentle lengthening for the back of the legs.

setup: Move into a light stretch. You should feel tension but not pain.
Keep your breathing slow and calm.
Hold posture tall so the stretch is targeted.
movement: Ease deeper only as your body relaxes.
Hold for 20 to 30 seconds and breathe.
Return slowly and reset before the next rep.
Repeat without bouncing.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel a gentle stretch in your hamstrings without joint discomfort.

commonFixes.fix: Hinge at the hips and keep your spine long.
Ease in and stop before sharp discomfort.

makeItEasier.name: Shorter Range
makeItEasier.description: Use a smaller range where you feel only mild tension.

levelUp.description: Hold each rep 10 to 15 seconds longer while keeping relaxed breathing.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in and out slowly as you ease into the stretch. Relax on the exhale.

name: Shoulder Dislocations (Band)
description: Smooth shoulder mobility with a band.

setup: Move into a light stretch. You should feel tension but not pain.
Keep your breathing slow and calm.
Hold posture tall so the stretch is targeted.
movement: Ease deeper only as your body relaxes.
Hold for 20 to 30 seconds and breathe.
Return slowly and reset before the next rep.
Repeat without bouncing.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel a gentle stretch in your shoulders without joint discomfort.

commonFixes.fix: Widen your grip so the stretch is smooth.
Keep ribs down and move from the shoulders.

makeItEasier.name: Shorter Range
makeItEasier.description: Use a smaller range where you feel only mild tension.

levelUp.description: Hold each rep 10 to 15 seconds longer while keeping relaxed breathing.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you bring the band forward, breathe out as you ease it over and behind.

name: Feet-Elevated Pike Push-Up
description: Shoulder-dominant pressing with feet raised to progress toward handstand strength.

setup: Place your feet on a bench or box and your hands on the floor, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Drive your hips up so your body forms an inverted V; head points toward the floor.
Brace your core and keep your legs straight or slightly bent.
movement: Bend your elbows and lower the top of your head toward the floor between your hands.
Keep the pike shape; avoid letting your hips drop or drift forward.
Press back up to the start with control.
Reset at the top before the next rep.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your shoulders and triceps working hard while your core keeps the pike shape stable.

commonFixes.issue: Hips sagging or losing the pike
commonFixes.fix: Push your hips high and keep your core tight so the V-shape stays constant.
Keep elbows at a comfortable angle, not straight out to the sides.

makeItEasier.name: Pike Push-Up
makeItEasier.description: Keep feet on the floor to reduce load while learning the movement.

levelUp.description: Raise your feet higher or progress to handstand push-ups against a wall.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower, breathe out as you press back up. Keep breathing steady.

name: Dorsal Raise
description: Prone hold that strengthens the lower back, glutes, and hamstrings for posture and posterior chain health.

setup: Lie face down with arms extended forward and legs straight.
Rest your forehead or chin lightly on the floor; keep your neck neutral.
Engage your core so only your abdomen and hips stay in contact with the floor.
movement: Lift your arms, chest, and legs a few inches off the floor at the same time.
Hold briefly at the top; keep a slight arch through your spine.
Lower with control and reset without relaxing fully between reps.
Repeat for the desired number of reps.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your lower back, glutes, and hamstrings working while your upper back and rear shoulders assist.

commonFixes.issue: Lifting too high and straining the lower back
commonFixes.fix: Lift only a few inches; focus on squeezing the muscles, not maximum height.
Breathe steadily; exhale as you lift, inhale as you lower.

makeItEasier.name: Alternating Dorsal Raise
makeItEasier.description: Lift one arm and the opposite leg at a time to reduce load.

levelUp.description: Hold the top position longer or add light ankle or hand weights.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out as you lift, breathe in as you lower. Keep breathing steady.

name: Hollow Hold
description: Foundational core and body-tension hold for strength and stability.

setup: Lie on your back with legs straight and arms extended overhead.
Lift your head, shoulders, and legs off the floor so only your lower back stays in contact.
Press your lower back into the floor and create a slight C-curve with your torso.
movement: Hold the hollow shape with arms and legs off the floor.
Keep your lower back pressed down; avoid letting it arch.
Breathe steadily without losing tension.
Stop when you can no longer keep the position or your back starts to lift.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel constant tension through your abs and hip flexors with your lower back staying flat on the floor.

commonFixes.issue: Lower back arching off the floor
commonFixes.fix: Tuck your pelvis and press the small of your back down; reduce the height of arms or legs if needed.
Take small, steady breaths while keeping the hollow shape.

makeItEasier.name: Bent-Knee Hollow Hold
makeItEasier.description: Bend your knees and bring them toward your chest to shorten the lever and reduce load.

levelUp.description: Extend legs lower, add rocks, or progress to hollow rocks for more challenge.

instructions.breathingCue: Exhale to brace and press your lower back down as you get into the hold, then breathe steadily without losing the hollow shape.

name: Hanging Knee Raise
description: Core and hip flexor strength from the bar with grip and stability work.

setup: Hang from a bar with an overhand grip, hands about shoulder-width apart.
Start with arms extended and shoulders engaged; avoid sinking into your shoulders.
Keep your legs straight or slightly bent at the start.
movement: Pull your knees up toward your chest by flexing your hips and engaging your core.
Pause briefly when your thighs are near horizontal or higher.
Lower your legs with control to the start position.
Avoid swinging; use your abs and hip flexors to control the movement.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your lower abs and hip flexors doing the work, with your lats and grip helping you stay stable.

commonFixes.fix: Slow down and pause at the top and bottom; keep your torso as still as possible.

commonFixes.issue: Shrugging or sinking in the shoulders
commonFixes.fix: Keep your shoulders packed down and away from your ears throughout the hang.

makeItEasier.name: Dead Hang
makeItEasier.description: Practice hanging for time to build grip and shoulder stability before adding the knee raise.

levelUp.description: Straighten your legs and raise them to horizontal or higher (hanging leg raise).

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out as you lift your knees, breathe in as you lower. Keep breathing steady.

name: Pilates
description: Low-impact core and flexibility work for stability and control.

setup: Choose a mat or padded surface.
Start with a few deep breaths to center yourself.
Move at a controlled pace; quality over speed.
movement: Follow a sequence of controlled movements targeting core, hips, and posture.
Keep your breath steady and avoid holding it.
Stop if you feel sharp pain or dizziness.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your core and stabilizers working without strain in your joints.

commonFixes.fix: Slow down and focus on one rep at a time with full control.
Breathe out on effort, breathe in on release; keep it rhythmic.

makeItEasier.name: Shorter session
makeItEasier.description: Do 10 to 15 minutes and build up as you feel ready.

levelUp.description: Extend session length or add resistance with bands or light weights.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out on effort, breathe in on release. Keep a steady rhythm.

name: Yoga
description: Flexibility, balance, and mindful movement for body and breath.

setup: Use a mat and wear comfortable clothes that allow movement.
Start with a few calm breaths.
Honor your range of motion; no forcing into poses.
movement: Move through poses at your own pace, linking breath with movement.
Keep joints in a safe range; back off if you feel sharp pain.
Include a short rest or savasana at the end if you like.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel stretch and engagement without strain; breathing stays steady.

commonFixes.fix: Focus on your own body; every practice is different.
Ease into positions and hold where you feel a gentle pull, not pain.

makeItEasier.name: Shorter flow
makeItEasier.description: Do 15 to 20 minutes or a few poses; consistency matters more than length.

levelUp.description: Lengthen your practice or try more challenging poses as you build strength and flexibility.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you expand or lengthen, breathe out as you fold or release. Keep it smooth and even.

name: Swimming
description: Full-body, low-impact cardio that builds endurance and works the whole body.

setup: Warm up with a few easy lengths or water walking.
Choose a stroke and pace you can sustain.
Stay in a lane or area appropriate for your speed.
movement: Swim at a steady pace; focus on smooth strokes and steady breathing.
Rest at the wall as needed; build duration gradually.
Cool down with slower laps or treading water.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your heart rate rise with minimal impact on your joints.

commonFixes.fix: Exhale steadily underwater and inhale when you turn; practice rhythm.
Keep strokes in line with your shoulders and recover smoothly.

makeItEasier.name: Water walking or kickboard
makeItEasier.description: Use the pool for walking or a kickboard to reduce arm load while building time in the water.

levelUp.description: Add distance, mix strokes, or include short intervals for more challenge.

instructions.breathingCue: Exhale in the water, inhale when you turn; find a rhythm that keeps you relaxed.

name: Bouldering
description: Climbing short routes without ropes; builds grip, back, and problem-solving.

setup: Warm up with easy traverses or a few simple problems.
Use appropriate climbing shoes and chalk if needed.
Land on padded flooring; be aware of others.
movement: Climb problems that match your level; rest between attempts.
Use your legs to push and your arms to hold and pull when needed.
Descend safely; downclimb or drop only when you can do so without risk.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your back, arms, and grip working; heart rate rises with effort.

commonFixes.fix: Drive with your legs and keep your arms straighter to save grip.
Use open hands and rest on holds where possible to preserve forearm stamina.

makeItEasier.name: Traversing
makeItEasier.description: Climb sideways along the wall at a comfortable height to build technique and endurance.

levelUp.description: Try harder problems, longer sessions, or add hangboard work on rest days.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out on hard moves, breathe in as you rest or reposition. Avoid holding your breath on cruxes.

name: Hiking
description: Walk on trails and terrain for cardio and time in nature.

setup: Choose a trail that matches your fitness and experience.
Wear supportive footwear and take water and snacks.
Start at an easy pace to warm up.
movement: Walk at a steady pace; use poles if they help with balance or load.
Take short breaks as needed; stay hydrated.
Pace yourself on climbs and watch your footing on descents.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your legs and heart working without pushing into breathless effort unless you choose to.

commonFixes.fix: Ease into the first 10 to 15 minutes so you can sustain the whole hike.
Shorten your steps on steep or uneven ground to stay stable.

makeItEasier.name: Flat or gentle trails
makeItEasier.description: Stick to easier terrain and shorter distances until you build stamina.

levelUp.description: Add distance, elevation, or pack weight as you get fitter.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily; use a rhythm that matches your steps on climbs.

name: Skiing
description: Downhill skiing for cardio and leg strength in winter conditions.

setup: Use properly fitted skis, boots, and a helmet.
Start on terrain you can handle; warm up with easy runs.
Stay in control and follow the skier responsibility code.
movement: Ski at a controlled pace; turn to manage speed and line.
Rest when your legs fatigue; stay hydrated.
Adjust to conditions and visibility; know when to call it a day.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your legs and core working; heart rate rises with effort and terrain.

commonFixes.fix: Keep weight forward over your boots and press your shins into the cuff.
Absorb bumps and terrain with your knees and ankles; stay loose.

makeItEasier.name: Green runs and short sessions
makeItEasier.description: Stay on easier slopes and limit run length until you build fitness and technique.

levelUp.description: Add steeper or longer runs, or more days per season, as you progress.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily; exhale on demanding turns or bumps to stay relaxed.

name: Road Cycling
description: Cycling on paved roads for sustained cardio and leg endurance.

setup: Wear a helmet and ensure your bike is roadworthy.
Start at an easy pace for 5 to 10 minutes to warm up.
Choose a route and distance that match your fitness.
movement: Pedal at a steady cadence; shift gears to keep effort manageable.
Stay visible and follow road rules; signal and scan for traffic.
Cool down with easy spinning for the last few minutes.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your legs driving and your heart rate up without gasping for breath.

commonFixes.fix: Use easier gears and higher cadence to spare your knees and build endurance.
Relax your shoulders and grip the bars lightly; keep your elbows slightly bent.

makeItEasier.name: Shorter, flatter rides
makeItEasier.description: Reduce distance and avoid big hills until you build base fitness.

levelUp.description: Add distance, climbing, or short intervals as you get stronger.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily; match breath to effort so you stay in a sustainable zone.

name: Mountain Biking
description: Off-road cycling for cardio, leg strength, and bike handling.

setup: Wear a helmet and protective gear appropriate for the trail.
Check your bike; brakes and tires in good shape.
Start on terrain you can handle and warm up on easier sections.
movement: Ride at a controlled pace; look ahead and choose your line.
Use your body to absorb bumps and balance; shift your weight as needed.
Rest when needed; stay hydrated and within your skill level.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your legs and heart working; core and arms help with control and stability.

commonFixes.fix: Relax your arms and grip; let the bike move under you over rough ground.
Stand and use your legs as suspension; keep pedals level when not pedaling.

makeItEasier.name: Green trails and fire roads
makeItEasier.description: Stick to easier, smoother trails until you build fitness and confidence.

levelUp.description: Progress to harder trails, longer rides, or more technical sections as you improve.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily; exhale on hard efforts or technical sections to stay loose.

name: Tennis
description: Racquet sport for cardio, agility, and full-body conditioning.

setup: Warm up with light hitting or jogging; stretch if that helps you.
Use a racket and court appropriate for your level.
Play singles or doubles; both count as session time.
movement: Play at a pace you can sustain; focus on moving your feet and recovering between shots.
Rest between games or sets as needed; stay hydrated.
Cool down with easy rallying or walking.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your heart rate up and your legs and shoulders working during play.

commonFixes.fix: Stay on the balls of your feet and take small steps to get in position.
Prepare early and turn your body so you contact the ball in front.

makeItEasier.name: Shorter sessions or doubles
makeItEasier.description: Play for a set time or choose doubles to share the court and reduce continuous running.

levelUp.description: Add duration, play more singles, or add drills for fitness and skill.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out on the hit, breathe in as you recover; keep a steady rhythm during rallies.

name: Rugby
description: Team sport combining running, contact, and skill for full-body cardio and strength.

setup: Warm up with running and dynamic stretches; ensure you have appropriate kit and mouthguard.
Play in a match, training, or touch/tag session.
Know the rules and play within your ability and the level of the game.
movement: Run, pass, and support; tackle and ruck only if you are trained and it is safe.
Rest when substituted or at half-time; stay hydrated.
Cool down and stretch after play; address any knocks or fatigue.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your heart rate high at times, with legs and core doing a lot of work.

commonFixes.fix: Keep your head up and body behind the ball; tackle only with proper technique.
Call for the ball and talk to teammates for support and safety.

makeItEasier.name: Touch or tag rugby
makeItEasier.description: Play non-contact versions to build fitness and skills with less impact.

levelUp.description: Increase match time, intensity, or move to full contact only when ready and coached.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out on effort in contact or sprints; recover with steady breaths when the ball is dead.

name: Badminton
description: Fast racquet sport for cardio, reflexes, and leg agility.

setup: Warm up with light hitting and footwork.
Use a racket and shuttlecock; play singles or doubles.
Start at a pace you can sustain for the session.
movement: Play rallies and move to the shuttle; recover to a central position between shots.
Rest between games; stay hydrated.
Cool down with easy hitting or walking.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your heart rate up and your legs and shoulder working during play.

commonFixes.fix: Use your feet to get to the shuttle so you can hit from a balanced position.
Grip firmly on the hit but relax between shots to save your arm and improve touch.

makeItEasier.name: Doubles or shorter games
makeItEasier.description: Play doubles or cap the session length to manage intensity.

levelUp.description: Play longer sessions, more singles, or add drills for fitness and technique.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out on the shot, breathe in as you recover; keep a steady rhythm.

name: Cricket
description: Bat and ball sport for cardio, coordination, and short bursts of effort.

setup: Warm up with light running and throwing; use appropriate kit including pads and helmet if batting.
Take part in a match, nets, or practice session.
Play in a format and level that matches your fitness and skill.
movement: Bat, bowl, or field; run between wickets and chase the ball as your role requires.
Rest when off the field or between overs; stay hydrated.
Cool down and stretch after play.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your heart rate rise during active phases; legs and shoulders do a lot of work.

commonFixes.fix: Turn quickly and run with short, sharp steps; communicate with your partner.
Stay on your toes and move early so you are balanced when you catch or throw.

makeItEasier.name: Nets or shorter formats
makeItEasier.description: Practice in nets or play shorter formats to build fitness and skill without long sessions.

levelUp.description: Play longer formats or take on more demanding roles as fitness and skill improve.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out on the shot or throw, breathe in as you recover; stay relaxed between balls.

name: Golf
description: Walking and swinging for light cardio, coordination, and time outdoors.

setup: Warm up with a few practice swings and short walks.
Walk the course or use a cart as you prefer; carry or pull clubs if you like the extra work.
Play at a pace that keeps you moving without rushing.
movement: Play holes at your own pace; walk between shots when you can.
Take your time over the ball; rest between holes if needed.
Cool down with a short walk or stretch after the round.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel light cardio from walking and some core and back use in the swing.

commonFixes.fix: Take a breath and a smooth practice swing before each shot.
Stay athletic and relaxed; bend from the hips and keep your spine angle consistent.

makeItEasier.name: Shorter course or par-3
makeItEasier.description: Play 9 holes or a par-3 course to reduce time and walking load.

levelUp.description: Walk 18 holes, carry your bag, or add a second round per week as fitness allows.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe out gently on the downswing; stay relaxed through the shot.

name: Snowboarding
description: Winter board sport for cardio and leg strength on the slopes.

setup: Wear a helmet and suitable winter gear; ensure your board and bindings are set for you.
Start on terrain you can handle; warm up with easy runs.
Stay in control and be aware of others on the slope.
movement: Ride at a controlled speed; use your edges to turn and slow down.
Rest when your legs tire; stay hydrated and watch the conditions.
Adjust to visibility and snow; know when to take a break.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel your legs and core working; heart rate rises with effort and terrain.

commonFixes.fix: Keep weight centered over the board and press into your front foot to steer.
Bend your knees and absorb bumps; let the board flex and respond.

makeItEasier.name: Green runs and short sessions
makeItEasier.description: Stay on easier slopes and limit time on the board until you build legs and confidence.

levelUp.description: Add steeper runs, longer days, or park features as you progress.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe steadily; exhale on demanding turns or landings to stay relaxed.

name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
description: Unilateral hinge for hamstrings and glutes with balance demand.
siblingReason: Unilateral hinge. Exposes and corrects left/right hamstring and glute asymmetries. Greater balance and proprioception demand than bilateral RDL. Useful when imbalance is suspected or as a progression from bilateral hinge.

setup: Stand on one leg with a soft bend in the working knee, holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand.
Hinge at the hip, letting the free leg extend behind you as a counterbalance.
Keep your hips square — resist the urge to rotate open toward the free side.
movement: Lower the dumbbell toward the floor by hinging at the hip, keeping your back flat and the weight close to your standing leg.
Lower until you feel a strong stretch in the hamstring of the working leg or until your hips begin to rotate.
Drive through the heel of the working foot to stand tall, squeezing the glute at the top.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel a strong stretch in the hamstring of the standing leg and a squeeze in the glute at the top. Your core should feel braced throughout to keep hips level.

commonFixes.fix: Think about keeping both hip bones pointing toward the floor throughout the movement. Slow the descent if rotation starts early.
Fix your gaze on a point on the floor about 2 metres ahead. Keep the movement slow and controlled — speed creates instability.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you hinge and lower, breathe out as you drive up to standing.

makeItEasier.name: Romanian Deadlift
makeItEasier.description: Build bilateral hinge strength and hamstring control before adding the balance challenge.

levelUp.description: Add a barbell or increase dumbbell load once balance and control are solid.

name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
description: Eccentric hamstring exercise with strong evidence for injury prevention.
siblingReason: The gold standard eccentric hamstring exercise with the strongest evidence base for hamstring injury prevention. Cannot be substituted by leg curl — the eccentric loading pattern is fundamentally different. Should be included in any programme where hamstring injury history or risk is present.

setup: Kneel on a mat with your feet anchored under a bar, partner holding them, or in a Nordic curl machine.
Keep your body upright from knees to head, core braced, and arms ready to catch yourself.
Your weight is supported through your knees — use padding if needed.
movement: Slowly lower your body toward the floor by letting your knees extend, resisting the fall with your hamstrings for as long as possible.
When you can no longer hold yourself, use your hands to catch the floor and control the landing.
Drive back up to the starting position by pulling with your hamstrings and pushing with your hands if needed.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel intense eccentric tension through the back of your thighs during the lowering phase. The burn will be significant — this is normal and expected.

commonFixes.fix: Keep your hips extended and body in a straight line from knees to head throughout. Hinging at the hip turns this into a hip hinge, not a Nordic curl.
The goal is maximum time under tension during the lowering phase. Aim for 3-5 seconds on the way down. Use hands to push back up until eccentric strength develops.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in as you lower under control, breathe out as you pull yourself back to the start.

makeItEasier.name: Lying Leg Curl
makeItEasier.description: Build basic hamstring strength before introducing Nordic eccentric loading.

levelUp.description: Progress to full reps without hand assistance, then add a weight plate to the chest.

name: Single-Leg Hip Thrust
description: Unilateral glute bridge for isolation and asymmetry correction.
siblingReason: Unilateral progression of hip thrust. Exposes glute asymmetries and dramatically increases the stimulus per rep by doubling the relative load on the working side. Use when bilateral hip thrust becomes insufficiently challenging or when asymmetry is identified.

setup: Sit on the floor with your upper back against the bench edge as you would for a standard hip thrust.
Extend one leg out straight, keeping the working foot flat on the floor with the knee bent.
Brace your core and tuck your chin slightly before driving.
movement: Drive through the heel of the working foot to extend your hips upward toward the ceiling.
Extend until your hips are fully extended and your body forms a straight line from shoulder to working knee.
Pause and squeeze the working glute hard at the top, then lower under control and repeat.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel a strong, isolated contraction in the glute of the working leg. The non-working leg should remain relaxed — if it is working hard, you are compensating.

commonFixes.fix: Focus on driving the hip straight up, not rotating. Slow down and reduce range of motion until control improves.
Move your working foot slightly further from your body to reduce hamstring involvement and increase glute demand.

instructions.breathingCue: Breathe in at the bottom, breathe out as you drive your hip up and squeeze the glute.

makeItEasier.name: Hip Thrust
makeItEasier.description: Build bilateral glute strength before progressing to single-leg loading.

levelUp.description: Add a dumbbell or plate to the working hip once bodyweight feels controlled.

name: Weighted Pull-Up
description: Pull-up with added load for continued strength and hypertrophy.
siblingReason: The primary progression beyond bodyweight pull-up. Allows continued strength and hypertrophy development once bodyweight reps exceed 8-10 with good form. Load added via belt, weighted vest, or dumbbell held between feet.

setup: Attach a weight via a dipping belt around your waist, or hold a dumbbell between your feet or ankles.
Hang from the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder width, arms fully extended.
Engage your lats and core before beginning the pull.
movement: Drive your elbows down and back to pull your chin over the bar.
Control the ascent — the added weight makes it easy to rush and lose form.
Lower under full control to a dead hang, feeling the lats lengthen before the next rep.
instructions.goodFormFeels: You should feel the same lat and upper back engagement as a bodyweight pull-up, with greater overall tension through the whole movement due to the load.

commonFixes.fix: Reduce the weight. Weighted pull-ups require strict form — momentum defeats the purpose of adding load.
Focus on keeping your shoulders depressed throughout. Think about pulling your shoulder blades down and together, not lifting toward your ears.
